blocks.text: DTC P0340: VTC Cam Sensor No pulse

The TDC sensor consists of a pulsar rotor and semiconductors that detect rotor position and intake camshaft timing. When the engine starts, the pulsar rotor rotates, causing the magnetic flux of the TDC sensor to fluctuate. The TDC sensor also detects the top dead center of each cylinder, and the PGM-FI ECU uses the pulse signals from the TDC sensor and the crank sensor to determine the intake camshaft position. If the pulse signal from the TDC sensor is not input, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
At least 2 seconds (at 750 rpm engine speed)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When no pulse signal is input from the TDC sensor during 300 consecutive detections of a pulse signal from the crank sensor.

items.text: Ground fault in code (TDCAM line) between PGM-FI ECU and TDC sensor
Disconnection of code (TDCAM line) between PGM-FI ECU and TDC sensor
Disconnection of TDC sensor cord (power supply line)
Disconnection of code (CRKSG line) between PGM-FI ECU and TDC sensor

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
